knowledge_base.topic: Compliance decouples stiffness from iteration count and time step
knowledge_base.insight: Replace stiffness k with compliance α = 1/k. Normalise as α̃ = α/Δt² to make behaviour independent of time step and iteration count.

knowledge_base.topic: Lagrange multiplier accumulation for force estimation
knowledge_base.insight: Accumulate Δλ across iterations. The constraint force is estimated as f ≈ λ/Δt, enabling reaction impulse measurement.

knowledge_base.source: Müller et al., Detailed Rigid Body Simulation (SCA 2020)
knowledge_base.topic: Two-way rigid-soft coupling via inverse mass weighting
knowledge_base.insight: Place rigid-body CoM as a particle with w = 1/m_body. Constraint corrections distribute proportionally to inverse masses, automatically producing Newton's Third Law reactions.

knowledge_base.source: Müller et al., Detailed Rigid Body Simulation (SCA 2020)
knowledge_base.insight: Update positions immediately after each constraint solve (not after full iteration). This makes the solver non-linear and avoids freezing constraint directions.

knowledge_base.source: Matthias Müller, Ten Minute Physics Tutorial 15
knowledge_base.insight: 1) Spatial hash table for O(1) queries. 2) rest_length >= 2*radius to avoid constraint fighting. 3) Sub-steps for stability. 4) v_max = 0.2*radius/Δt_sub for tunnelling guard. 5) Friction damping after collision response.

knowledge_base.topic: Rayleigh damping via compliance-like parameter β
knowledge_base.insight: Damping is modelled as γ = α̃·β/Δt. The modified update Δλ = (-C - α̃·λ - γ·Ċ·Δt) / ((1+γ)·∇C·M⁻¹·∇Cᵀ + α̃) provides stable energy dissipation without ad-hoc velocity scaling.
